description: Add real-time admin notification when a customer creates a support ticket via the portal. The backend needs a notification broadcast channel so that when a ticket is created by a customer (senderType='customer'), a WebSocket notification message is sent to all connected admin clients. The frontend realtime store already handles type='notification' messages.
steps: In panel/panel/internal/api/api.go: Add a notification broadcast channel to the Server struct. Add a field like `notifChan chan NotificationMsg` and a `wsSubscribers` sync map or slice to track connected admin WS writers.
Define a NotificationMsg struct: { ID string, Type string, Message string, Timestamp time.Time }.
In the Server constructor (New function), initialize the notifChan (buffered channel, e.g. 100).
In the realtimeWS handler, register the connection's writeRealtime function into a subscriber list. On disconnect (done channel), remove it. Use a sync.RWMutex-protected slice of callback functions.
Add a method `func (s *Server) broadcastNotification(msg NotificationMsg)` that iterates over all subscribers and writes the notification. Use non-blocking send to avoid slow clients blocking others.
In the createTicket function: after successful commit, if senderType is 'customer', call s.broadcastNotification with a message like 'New support ticket: <subject>' from the customer username.
In the realtimeWS goroutine loop, add a case for receiving from the notification channel (or better: use the subscriber pattern where each WS connection has its own channel that receives broadcasts).

Alternative simpler approach: Instead of a full pub/sub, add a 'last_notification_id' tracker. On each 3-second tick in realtimeWS, also query for recent notifications (last 5 seconds) and send them. This avoids complex channel management. Query: SELECT from a notifications table or use an in-memory ring buffer.

Simplest approach that works: Add a sync.RWMutex-protected slice of notification channels (one per WS connection) to the Server struct. broadcastNotification sends to all channels. Each realtimeWS goroutine listens on its channel in the select loop.
Test that go build ./... compiles successfully after changes.
acceptance_criteria: When a customer creates a ticket via /api/portal/tickets (POST), connected admin WebSocket clients receive a notification message
The notification message has type='notification' and includes ticket subject and customer username
The admin frontend realtime store already processes these and shows them in the notification count badge
findings: Implemented pub/sub notification broadcast using sync.RWMutex-protected slice of channels. Each admin WS connection subscribes on connect and unsubscribes on disconnect. When a customer creates a ticket (senderType='customer'), broadcastNotification sends to all channels with non-blocking send. Build and all tests pass.
